extracted_information: This document provides a comprehensive comparison between CodeMirror and Monaco Editor, detailing their differences in bundle size, architecture, features, performance, development experience, mobile support, and accessibility. It also offers guidance on when to choose each editor based on specific project requirements.

Monaco Editor.architecture: Traditional approach with global configuration and shared state, primarily built around VS Code’s architecture.
Monaco Editor.performance: Optimized for handling large codebases (100k+ lines) through viewport-aware tokenization and incremental model updates. Can impact initial load times, especially on resource-constrained devices.
Monaco Editor.development_experience: Immediate, feature-rich experience similar to VS Code, with sophisticated IntelliSense, code linting, syntax highlighting, and integrated debugging.

CodeMirror 6.architecture: Functional core with imperative shell architecture, built around immutable state and pure functions. Uses 'facets' for its extension system, allowing modularity and efficient tree-shaking.
CodeMirror 6.performance: Focuses on avoiding performance cliffs, uses efficient data structures (tree-based document representation, viewport-based rendering) to remain responsive with gigantic documents by doing only necessary work.
CodeMirror 6.development_experience: Requires more initial configuration but offers superior flexibility; modular design allows precisely tailored editing experiences through its extension system.

features.CodeMirror 6: First-class mobile responsiveness, superior mobile support with native platform integration

features.Monaco Editor: Good features including font size adjustment, high contrast themes, comprehensive keyboard shortcuts
features.CodeMirror 6: Built-in features, works well with screen readers, supports keyboard-only navigation
